title: No Funding for Illegal Migrant Billboards Act
short_title: No Funding for Illegal Migrant Billboards Act
stage_label: Referred to the Subcommittee on Border Security and Enforcement.
plain_summary: This bill stops the Department of Homeland Security from using government money to advertise a special office called the Immigration Detention Ombudsman. This office helps people who are being held in immigration detention centers and looks into problems at these facilities. The bill would prevent the government from paying for billboards, ads, or other ways to tell the public about this office and what it does. The office would still exist, but the government could not spend money to advertise it to people.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Border Security and Enforcement.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the No Funding for Illegal Migrant Billboards Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Prohibition on Certain Advertising with Respect to the Immigration Detention Ombudsman
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called the Homeland Security Act of two thousand two. It adds a new rule that says the Secretary of Homeland Security cannot spend any government money to advertise the Immigration Detention Ombudsman to the general public. This means no billboards or other public advertising can be paid for with government funds to tell people about this office or what it does. The Immigration Detention Ombudsman is an official who helps people who are being held in immigration detention facilities.
